channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Pokémon Champions Twitch Rivals: All You Need to Know
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Champions will have its first official tournament this week in Twitch Rivals.

The event will see content creators battle each other in doubles for a share of the $50k prize pool.

There will be quite a mix of participating streamers, with 16 participants in total.

These include popular VTuber shxtuo and former Achievement Hunter and Rooster Teeth member Matt’ AxialMatt’ Bragg.

Here is everything you need to know about the upcoming Pokémon Champions event.

How to Watch Pokémon Champions Twitch Rivals?

The entire tournament will be streamed live on the TwitchRivals Twitch channel.

On the primary stream, the tournament will be commentated by Adam Save, Aaron ‘Cybertron’ Zheng, and Sierra Dawn.

Each creator’s perspective will also be viewable on their individual Twitch channels.

The event will take place on April 29 at 14:00 PDT/ 17:00 EDT.

Pokémon Champions Twitch Rivals Streamers

  • shxtou (1.4m followers)
  • EsfandTV (1.4m followers)
  • Risshella (77.5k followers)
  • chrismelberger (139k followers)
  • AxialMatt (114k followers)
  • partyarlie (72.7k followers)
  • Dekkster (146k followers)
  • MeesterKeem (43.4k followers)
  • JonSandman (485k followers)
  • MomoMisfortune (27k followers)
  • AshSaidHi (22.9k followers)
  • prncessdiana (23k followers)
  • Kwikpanik (12.3k followers)
  • GlitchxCity (22.5k followers)
  • DataDave (63.9k followers)
  • sunnie (16.2k followers)

Pokémon Champions Twitch Rivals Format

The 16 players will be split into groups of four to decide seeding before a single-elimination bracket determines the champion.

The whole tournament will be played in doubles and will feature best-of-ones except for the final, which will be a best-of-three.

The $50k prize pool will be distributed as follows:

1st – $3,500
2nd – $3,400
3rd – $3,350
4th – $3,250
5th – 6th $3,150
7th – 8th $3,100
9th – 12th $3,050
13th – 16th $2,950

Which creator will you be rooting for? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see why competitive Pokemon’s biggest creator WolfeyVGC defended Pokémon Champions’ limited roster.

MOBILE APP

item.title: 2K Reportedly Adds 14-Day Online Check For Games After Denuvo Crack
item.content:encoded:
2K Games wants to make sure you actually own the game you’re playing, and that you aren’t trying to bypass any anti-cheating measures that have been put in place. After reports surfaced that hackers found a way to get around Denuvo’s checks entirely, it appears that 2K is working to make sure there’s no way around it.

New claims suggest that 2K and Denuvo have worked together on a response that will require mandatory online check-ins for several 2K titles.

According to the claim, first reported by Tom’s Hardware and Pirat Nation, games like NBA 2K26, NBA 2K25, and Marvel Midnight Suns will use authorization tokens. These tokens will only last for 14 days, forcing players to log in to get a new token every time. If you don’t have one, it’s said, your game simply won’t launch, even if you own a genuine copy.

  • More DRM: PlayStation DRM Bug Sparks Fears Over Digital Game Access

“This is impossible for the HVB (hypervisor-based bypass) to emulate, as it’s a request/response call to Denuvo’s servers and thus in practice can’t be replicated,” Tom’s Hardware’s report reads. “At some point, the code that executes this check could be removed, but that requires a full game crack rather than the HBP.”

Pirat Nation adds, “The requirement is not clearly disclosed on Steam store pages, in the EULA, or at the time of purchase, meaning many buyers were unaware they would need periodic internet access even for single-player modes.”

Insider Gaming has reached out to 2K for comment on these claims and whether these new checks are permanent. Should a response be received, it will be added to this story.

What do you think of a mandatory 14-day online check-in for games from 2K and Denuvo? Leave your thoughts down in the comments and in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about the game director of Assassin’s Creed Hexe recently leaving the company. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Every FGC Tournament at EVO Japan 2026
item.content:encoded:
EVO Japan 2026 begins May 1 with the best fighting game players in the world competing across multiple events.

As one of the biggest events for the fighting game community (FGC), this year’s tournament will feature at least 12 tournaments, including Street Fighter, Tekken, 2XKO, and Guilty Gear.

In addition to major prize pools, a lot of these events will act as qualifiers for the Esports World Cup and other major fighting game tournaments.

EVO Japan 2026 Prize Pool

In addition to each tournament’s individual prize pool, EVO Japan will also give ¥30,000,000 (around $200k) in prize money spread out across all the events.

EVO Japan will boost the prize pools of every game, with more going towards the titles that attract the most sign-ups.

The top eight of every tournament will receive this prize money, with the winner of the most popular tournament receiving an extra ¥4,000,000 ($25k).

EVO Japan 2026 Headline Games

Street Fighter 6

Street Fighter 6 is possibly the biggest fighting game today, as it helped shatter EVO Japan’s sign-up record last year.

Of the 8,795 participants, over 1000 more than any previous iteration of the event, more than 6,500 were SF6 players.

This year’s SF6 event at EVO Japan will feature a $44k prize pool and is part of both the Esports World Cup and Capcom Pro Tour.

The winner will qualify for EWC 2026 and the highly prestigious Capcom Cup 13.

Tekken 8

Another of the major titles in the FGC, the best Tekken 8 players will also be in attendance at EVO Japan.

The tournament will feature a $30k prize pool and will also see the top four qualify for the big-money Esports World Cup.

Plus, the EVO Champion 2026 Tekken 8 champion will also advance to the Tekken World Tour Finals.

2XKO

One of the newer games on this list, with a strong history in esports, Riot Games is taking 2XKO’s competitive scene very seriously.

The 2XKO event at EVO Japan will feature a massive $87k prize pool, boosted by the Frame Perfect in-game skin bundle.

This will be the second 2XKO Major of the year, with a third coming up at EVO Las Vegas in June.

Fatal Fury City of Wolves

Fatal Fury’s competitive scene will continue at EVO Japan with a significant $40k prize pool.

The event will also feed into the SNK World Championship circuit and will see the top four qualify for this year’s Esports World Cup.

Guilty Gear Strive

Guilty Gear Strive was released back in 2021, yet the game has changed significantly following the 2.0 update.

The event is a staple of EVO Japan and will feature a $20k prize pool.

Granblue Fantasy Versus Rising

Another title from Arc System, Granblue Fantasy Versus Rising will also feature at EVO Japan with a $20k prize pool of its own.

Other Expected Games

  • Vampire Saviour
  • King of Fighters XV
  • Virtua Fighter 5 R.E.V.O
  • Melty Blood Type Lumina
  • Under Night In-Birth II [SYS:Celes]
  • Hokuto no Ken

What event at EVO Japan 2026 are you most excited for? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And in other FGC tournament news, Red Bull announced ‘Knockout’ a new country-based 2XKO event.

MOBILE APP
